revision.text: #redirect[[en:Prince_Sakurai]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''大原 桜井'''（おおはら の さくらい、生没年不詳）は、[[奈良時代]]の[[皇族]]・[[貴族]]・[[歌人]]。もと'''桜井王'''を称し、[[臣籍降下]]後の[[氏姓]]は[[大原氏|大原真人]]。[[敏達天皇]]の後裔<ref>『新撰姓氏録』左京皇別</ref><ref name="o">「音石山大僧都伝」『日本高僧伝要文抄』第3</ref>、[[筑紫大宰帥]]・[[河内王]]の子。[[官位]]は[[従四位|従四位下]]・[[弾正台|弾正尹]]。
== 経歴 == [[和銅]]7年（[[714年]]）無位から[[従五位|従五位下]]に直叙される。[[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）従五位上。同年に[[井上内親王]]が[[斎王]]に卜定されて[[伊勢神宮]]への[[奉幣]]を行った際、[[橘諸兄|葛城王]]らとともに輿長を務めている<ref>『政事要略』巻24</ref>。
[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]）[[聖武天皇]]の[[即位]]後間もなく[[正五位|正五位下]]に叙せられると、神亀6年（[[729年]]）正五位上、[[天平]]3年（[[730年]]）[[従四位|従四位下]]と聖武朝前半にて順調に昇進した。またこの頃、[[侍従]]として聖武天皇に仕えた兄弟と想定される[[大原門部|門部王]]ら10余人とともに「風流侍従」と称された<ref>『藤氏家伝』武智麻呂伝</ref>。
天平11年（[[739年]]）兄弟の[[大原高安|高安王]]・門部王らとともに[[大原氏|大原真人]]姓を賜与され[[臣籍降下]]する。[[大蔵省#律令制における大蔵省|大蔵卿]]在任時の天平16年（[[744年]]）には、聖武天皇の難波宮行幸に際して、[[恭仁京]]の留守司に任ぜられている。
のち、[[弾正台|弾正尹]]も務め、『[[万葉集]]』に採録された[[和歌]]によると<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref>、[[孝謙天皇|孝謙]]朝の[[天平勝宝]]8年（[[756年]]）頃まで存命であったとされる<ref name="oz">尾崎[2009: 10]</ref>。
== 人物 == 万葉歌人として、[[天平]]5年（[[732年]]）頃以降の<ref>村山[1978: 7]</ref>[[遠江国#国司|遠江守]]在任時に[[聖武天皇]]と贈答した[[和歌]]と<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref>、[[天平勝宝]]8年（[[756年]]）頃に<ref name="oz" />[[佐保川]]の辺で詠んだ和歌<ref name="m4478" />が『[[万葉集]]』に採録されている。
== 官歴 == 注記のないものは『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *[[和銅]]7年（[[714年]]） 正月5日：[[従五位|従五位下]]（直叙） *[[養老]]5年（[[721年]]） 正月5日：従五位上 *[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]） 2月22日：[[正五位|正五位下]] *神亀6年（[[729年]]） 3月4日：正五位上 *[[天平]]3年（[[730年]]） 正月27日：[[従四位|従四位下]] *時期不詳：[[遠江国#国司|遠江守]]<ref name="m8" /> *天平11年（[[739年]]） 4月3日：[[大原氏|大原真人]]姓を賜与され[[臣籍降下]] *時期不詳：[[大蔵省#律令制における大蔵省|大蔵卿]] *天平16年（[[744年]]） 2月2日：[[恭仁京]]留守司 *時期不詳：[[弾正台|弾正尹]]<ref name="o" />
== 系譜 == *父：[[河内王]]<ref>『本朝皇胤紹運録』</ref> *母：不詳 *妻：不詳 **男子：大原真福<ref name="h55">鈴木真年『百家系図』巻55,大原眞人</ref> **男子：大原石本<ref name="h55" /> **男子：大原嗣麻呂<ref name="h55" /> **男子：大原年継<ref name="h55" />
== 脚注 == {{reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1995年 *村山出「「風流侍従」覚書 経歴の検討」『帯広大谷短期大学紀要 15』1978年 *尾崎富義「桜井王と聖武天皇の贈答歌」『常葉学園短期大学紀要 40』2009年 *[[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおはら の さくらい}} [[Category:奈良時代の男性皇族]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:大原氏|さくらい]] [[Category:7世紀生]] [[Category:8世紀没]]

'''大原 桜井'''（おおはら の さくらい、生没年不詳）は、[[奈良時代]]の[[皇族]]・[[貴族]]・[[歌人]]。もと'''桜井王'''を称し、[[臣籍降下]]後の[[氏姓]]は[[大原氏|大原真人]]。[[敏達天皇]]の後裔<ref>『新撰姓氏録』左京皇別</ref><ref name="o">「音石山大僧都伝」『日本高僧伝要文抄』第3</ref>、[[筑紫大宰帥]]・[[河内王]]の子。[[官位]]は[[従四位|従四位下]]・[[弾正台|弾正尹]]。
== 経歴 == [[和銅]]7年（[[714年]]）無位から[[従五位|従五位下]]に直叙される。[[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）従五位上。同年に[[井上内親王]]が[[斎王]]に卜定されて[[伊勢神宮]]への[[奉幣]]を行った際、[[橘諸兄|葛城王]]らとともに輿長を務めている<ref>『政事要略』巻24</ref>。
[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]）[[聖武天皇]]の[[即位]]後間もなく[[正五位|正五位下]]に叙せられると、神亀6年（[[729年]]）正五位上、[[天平]]3年（[[730年]]）[[従四位|従四位下]]と聖武朝前半にて順調に昇進した。またこの頃、[[侍従]]として聖武天皇に仕えた兄弟と想定される[[大原門部|門部王]]ら10余人とともに「風流侍従」と称された<ref>『藤氏家伝』武智麻呂伝</ref>。
天平11年（[[739年]]）兄弟の[[大原高安|高安王]]・門部王らとともに[[大原氏|大原真人]]姓を賜与され[[臣籍降下]]する。[[大蔵省#律令制における大蔵省|大蔵卿]]在任時の天平16年（[[744年]]）には、聖武天皇の難波宮行幸に際して、[[恭仁京]]の留守司に任ぜられている。
のち、[[弾正台|弾正尹]]も務め、『[[万葉集]]』に採録された[[和歌]]によると<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref>、[[孝謙天皇|孝謙]]朝の[[天平勝宝]]8年（[[756年]]）頃まで存命であったとされる<ref name="oz">尾崎[2009: 10]</ref>。
== 人物 == 万葉歌人として、[[天平]]5年（[[732年]]）頃以降の<ref>村山[1978: 7]</ref>[[遠江国#国司|遠江守]]在任時に[[聖武天皇]]と贈答した[[和歌]]と<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref>、[[天平勝宝]]8年（[[756年]]）頃に<ref name="oz" />[[佐保川]]の辺で詠んだ和歌<ref name="m4478" />が『[[万葉集]]』に採録されている。
== 官歴 == 注記のないものは『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *[[和銅]]7年（[[714年]]） 正月5日：[[従五位|従五位下]]（直叙） *[[養老]]5年（[[721年]]） 正月5日：従五位上 *[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]） 2月22日：[[正五位|正五位下]] *神亀6年（[[729年]]） 3月4日：正五位上 *[[天平]]3年（[[730年]]） 正月27日：[[従四位|従四位下]] *時期不詳：[[遠江国#国司|遠江守]]<ref name="m8" /> *天平11年（[[739年]]） 4月3日：[[大原氏|大原真人]]姓を賜与され[[臣籍降下]] *時期不詳：[[大蔵省#律令制における大蔵省|大蔵卿]] *天平16年（[[744年]]） 2月2日：[[恭仁京]]留守司 *時期不詳：[[弾正台|弾正尹]]<ref name="o" />
== 系譜 == *父：[[河内王]]<ref>『本朝皇胤紹運録』</ref> *母：不詳 *妻：不詳 **男子：大原真福<ref name="h55">鈴木真年『百家系図』巻55,大原眞人</ref> **男子：大原石本<ref name="o" /> **男子：大原嗣麻呂<ref name="h55" /> **男子：大原年継<ref name="h55" />
== 脚注 == {{reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1995年 *村山出「「風流侍従」覚書 経歴の検討」『帯広大谷短期大学紀要 15』1978年 *尾崎富義「桜井王と聖武天皇の贈答歌」『常葉学園短期大学紀要 40』2009年 *[[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年 {{親王の一}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおはら の さくらい}} [[Category:賜姓皇族]] [[Category:奈良時代の男性皇族]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:万葉歌人]] [[Category:大原氏|さくらい]] [[Category:7世紀生]] [[Category:8世紀没]]

== 系譜 == *父：[[河内王]]<ref>『本朝皇胤紹運録』</ref> *母：不詳 *妻：不詳 **男子：大原真福<ref name="h55">鈴木真年『百家系図』巻55,大原眞人</ref> **男子：大原石本<ref name="o" /> **男子：大原嗣麻呂<ref name="h55" /> **男子：大原年継<ref name="h55" />
== 脚注 == {{reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1995年 *村山出「「風流侍従」覚書 経歴の検討」『帯広大谷短期大学紀要 15』1978年 *尾崎富義「桜井王と聖武天皇の贈答歌」『常葉学園短期大学紀要 40』2009年 *[[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおはら の さくらい}} [[Category:大原氏|さくらい]] [[Category:賜姓皇族]] [[Category:奈良時代の男性皇族]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:万葉歌人]] [[Category:7世紀生]] [[Category:8世紀没]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 大原桜井 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[奈良時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = 桜井王→大原桜井 | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[従四位|従四位下]][[弾正尹]] | 主君 = [[元明天皇]]→[[元正天皇]]→[[聖武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[大原氏|大原]][[真人]] | 父母 = 父：[[河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)|河内王]] | 兄弟 = [[大原高安|高安]]、'''桜井'''、[[大原門部|門部]] | 妻 = | 子 = 真福、石本、嗣麻呂、年継 | 特記事項 = }}
'''大原 桜井'''（おおはら の さくらい）は、[[奈良時代]]の[[皇族]]・[[貴族]]・[[歌人]]。もと'''桜井王'''を称し、[[臣籍降下]]後の[[氏姓制度|氏姓]]は[[大原氏|大原]][[真人]]。[[敏達天皇]]の後裔<ref>『新撰姓氏録』左京皇別</ref><ref name="o">「音石山大僧都伝」『日本高僧伝要文抄』第3</ref>、[[大宰帥|筑紫大宰帥]]・[[河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)|河内王]]の子。[[官位]]は[[従四位|従四位下]]・[[弾正尹]]。
== 経歴 == [[和銅]]7年（[[714年]]）無位から[[従五位|従五位下]]に直叙される。[[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）従五位上。同年に[[井上内親王]]が[[斎王]]に卜定されて[[伊勢神宮]]への[[奉幣]]を行った際、[[橘諸兄|葛城王]]らと共に輿長を務めている<ref>『政事要略』巻24</ref>。
[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]）[[聖武天皇]]の即位後間もなく[[正五位|正五位下]]に叙せられると、神亀6年（[[729年]]）正五位上、[[天平]]3年（[[730年]]）[[従四位|従四位下]]と聖武朝前半にて順調に昇進した。またこの頃、[[侍従]]として聖武天皇に仕えた兄弟と想定される[[大原門部|門部王]]ら10余人と共に「風流侍従」と称された<ref>『藤氏家伝』武智麻呂伝</ref>。
天平11年（[[739年]]）兄弟の[[大原高安|高安王]]・門部王らと共に大原真人姓を賜与され臣籍降下する。[[大蔵省#律令制における大蔵省|大蔵卿]]在任時の天平16年（[[744年]]）には、聖武天皇の難波宮行幸に際して、[[恭仁京]]の留守司に任ぜられている。
のち、弾正尹も務め、『[[万葉集]]』に採録された[[和歌]]によると<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref>、[[孝謙天皇|孝謙]]朝の[[天平勝宝]]8歳（[[756年]]）頃まで存命であったとされる<ref name="oz">尾崎[2009: 10]</ref>。
== 人物 == 万葉歌人として、[[天平]]5年（[[732年]]）頃以降の<ref>村山[1978: 7]</ref>[[遠江国#国司|遠江守]]在任時に[[聖武天皇]]と贈答した和歌と<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref>、天平勝宝8年（756年）頃に<ref name="oz" />[[佐保川]]の辺で詠んだ和歌<ref name="m4478" />が『万葉集』に採録されている。
== 官歴 == 注記のないものは『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *[[和銅]]7年（[[714年]]） 正月5日：[[従五位|従五位下]]（直叙） *[[養老]]5年（[[721年]]） 正月5日：従五位上 *[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]） 2月22日：[[正五位|正五位下]] *神亀6年（[[729年]]） 3月4日：正五位上 *[[天平]]3年（[[730年]]） 正月27日：[[従四位|従四位下]] *時期不詳：[[遠江国#国司|遠江守]]<ref name="m8" /> *天平11年（[[739年]]） 4月3日：[[大原氏|大原真人]]姓を賜与され[[臣籍降下]] *時期不詳：[[大蔵省#律令制における大蔵省|大蔵卿]] *天平16年（[[744年]]） 2月2日：[[恭仁京]]留守司 *時期不詳：[[弾正尹]]<ref name="o" />
== 系譜 == *父：[[河内王]]<ref>『本朝皇胤紹運録』</ref> *母：不詳 *妻：不詳 **男子：大原真福<ref name="h55">鈴木真年『百家系図』巻55,大原眞人</ref> **男子：大原石本<ref name="o" /> **男子：[[大原継麻呂]]<ref name="h55" /> **男子：大原年継<ref name="h55" />
== 脚注 == {{reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1995年 *村山出「「風流侍従」覚書 経歴の検討」『帯広大谷短期大学紀要 15』1978年 *尾崎富義「桜井王と聖武天皇の贈答歌」『常葉学園短期大学紀要 40』2009年 *[[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 大原桜井 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[奈良時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = 桜井王→大原桜井 | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[従四位|従四位下]][[弾正尹]] | 主君 = [[元明天皇]]→[[元正天皇]]→[[聖武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[大原氏|大原]][[真人]] | 父母 = 父：[[河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)|河内王]] | 兄弟 = [[大原高安|高安]]、'''桜井'''、[[大原門部|門部]] | 妻 = | 子 = 真福、石本、嗣麻呂、年継 | 特記事項 = }} '''大原 桜井'''（おおはら の さくらい）は、[[奈良時代]]の[[皇族]]・[[貴族]]・[[歌人]]。もと'''桜井王'''を称し、[[臣籍降下]]後の[[氏姓制度|氏姓]]は[[大原氏|大原]][[真人]]。[[敏達天皇]]の後裔<ref>『新撰姓氏録』左京皇別</ref><ref name="o">「音石山大僧都伝」『日本高僧伝要文抄』第3</ref>、[[大宰帥|筑紫大宰帥]]・[[河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)|河内王]]の子。[[官位]]は[[従四位|従四位下]]・[[弾正尹]]。
== 経歴 == [[和銅]]7年（[[714年]]）無位から[[従五位|従五位下]]に直叙される。[[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）従五位上。同年に[[井上内親王]]が[[斎王]]に卜定されて[[伊勢神宮]]への[[奉幣]]を行った際、[[橘諸兄|葛城王]]らと共に輿長を務めている<ref>『政事要略』巻24</ref>。
[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]）[[聖武天皇]]の即位後間もなく[[正五位|正五位下]]に叙せられると、神亀6年（[[729年]]）正五位上、[[天平]]3年（[[730年]]）[[従四位|従四位下]]と聖武朝前半にて順調に昇進した。またこの頃、[[侍従]]として聖武天皇に仕えた兄弟と想定される[[大原門部|門部王]]ら10余人と共に「風流侍従」と称された<ref>『藤氏家伝』武智麻呂伝</ref>。
天平11年（[[739年]]）兄弟の[[大原高安|高安王]]・門部王らと共に大原真人姓を賜与され臣籍降下する。[[大蔵卿]]在任時の天平16年（[[744年]]）には、聖武天皇の難波宮行幸に際して、[[恭仁京]]の留守司に任ぜられている。
のち、弾正尹も務め、『[[万葉集]]』に採録された[[和歌]]によると<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref>、[[孝謙天皇|孝謙]]朝の[[天平勝宝]]8歳（[[756年]]）頃まで存命であったとされる<ref name="oz">尾崎[2009: 10]</ref>。
== 人物 == 万葉歌人として、[[天平]]5年（[[732年]]）頃以降の<ref>村山[1978: 7]</ref>[[遠江国#国司|遠江守]]在任時に[[聖武天皇]]と贈答した和歌と<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref>、天平勝宝8年（756年）頃に<ref name="oz" />[[佐保川]]の辺で詠んだ和歌<ref name="m4478" />が『万葉集』に採録されている。
== 官歴 == 注記のないものは『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *[[和銅]]7年（[[714年]]） 正月5日：[[従五位|従五位下]]（直叙） *[[養老]]5年（[[721年]]） 正月5日：従五位上 *[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]） 2月22日：[[正五位|正五位下]] *神亀6年（[[729年]]） 3月4日：正五位上 *[[天平]]3年（[[730年]]） 正月27日：[[従四位|従四位下]] *時期不詳：[[遠江国#国司|遠江守]]<ref name="m8" /> *天平11年（[[739年]]） 4月3日：[[大原氏|大原真人]]姓を賜与され[[臣籍降下]] *時期不詳：[[大蔵卿]] *天平16年（[[744年]]） 2月2日：[[恭仁京]]留守司 *時期不詳：[[弾正尹]]<ref name="o" />
== 系譜 == *父：[[河内王]]<ref>『本朝皇胤紹運録』</ref> *母：不詳 *妻：不詳 **男子：大原石本<ref name="o" />
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1995年 *村山出「「風流侍従」覚書 経歴の検討」『帯広大谷短期大学紀要 15』1978年 *尾崎富義「桜井王と聖武天皇の贈答歌」『常葉学園短期大学紀要 40』2009年 *[[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおはら の さくらい}} [[Category:大原氏|さくらい]] [[Category:賜姓皇族]] [[Category:奈良時代の男性皇族]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:万葉歌人]] [[Category:従四位下受位者]] [[Category:7世紀生]] [[Category:8世紀没]]

contributor.username: ja>学者気取りの先頭はここで終わり！

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真..."
revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原 桜井) was a [[Nara period]] [[royal family|prince]], [[noble]], and [[waka poet]]. Originally named '''Sakurai no Ōkimi''' (桜井王), he adopted the name {{ill|Ōhara Clan|ja|大原氏}} upon relinquishing his royal status. He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『新撰姓氏録』左京皇別</ref><ref name="o">「音石山大僧都伝」『日本高僧伝要文抄』第3</ref> and the son of {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}}, a former {{ill|Governor-General of Tsukushi|ja|筑紫大宰帥}}. He held the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下) and served as [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹).
== Career ==
In [[Wadō]] 7 (714), Sakurai was promoted directly from no rank to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下). By [[Yōrō]] 5 (721), he reached [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従五位上). That same year, he served as a [[kago|palankin bearer]] alongside {{ill|Katsuragi no Ōkimi|ja|橘諸兄}} during the {{ill|ritual for Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王の卜定}} as the {{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.<ref>『政事要略』巻24</ref>
After [[Emperor Shōmu]]’s accession in [[Shinki]] 1 (724), Sakurai was appointed [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (正五位下). He continued his rise under Emperor Shōmu, reaching [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] in [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730). Around this period, Sakurai and his brothers, including {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}}, were known as the "Elegant Retainers" (風流侍従).<ref>『藤氏家伝』武智麻呂伝</ref>
In [[Tenpyō]] 11 (739), Sakurai was granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara|ja|大原氏}}, transitioning to commoner status. As {{ill|Treasurer|ja|大蔵卿}} in [[Tenpyō]] 16 (744), he was tasked with overseeing the {{ill|Kuni Palace|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu’s visit to the {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}. Later, he served as [[Danjō no In]].
He was still active during [[Empress Kōken]]'s reign in [[Tenpyō Shōhō]] 8 (756), evidenced by his poems included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}.<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref><ref name="oz">尾崎[2009: 10]</ref>
== Poetry ==
As a poet, Sakurai composed waka included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}. Notable poems include exchanges with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as {{ill|Governor of Tōtōmi|ja|遠江守}} around [[Tenpyō]] 5 (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a poem about {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around [[Tenpyō Shōhō]] 8 (756).<ref name="m4478" />
== Timeline ==

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原 桜井) was a [[Nara period]] [[royal family|prince]], [[noble]], and [[waka poet]]. Originally named '''Sakurai no Ōkimi''' (桜井王), he adopted the name {{ill|Ōhara Clan|ja|大原氏}} upon relinquishing his royal status. He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『新撰姓氏録』左京皇別</ref><ref name="o">「音石山大僧都伝」『日本高僧伝要文抄』第3</ref> and the son of {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}}, a former {{ill|Governor-General of Tsukushi|ja|筑紫大宰帥}}. He held the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下) and served as [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹).
== Career ==
In [[Wadō]] 7 (714), Sakurai was promoted directly from no rank to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下). By [[Yōrō]] 5 (721), he reached [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従五位上). That same year, he served as a [[kago|palankin bearer]] alongside {{ill|Katsuragi no Ōkimi|ja|橘諸兄}} during the {{ill|ritual for Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王の卜定}} as the {{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.<ref>『政事要略』巻24</ref>
After [[Emperor Shōmu]]’s accession in [[Shinki]] 1 (724), Sakurai was appointed [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (正五位下). He continued his rise under Emperor Shōmu, reaching [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] in [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730). Around this period, Sakurai and his brothers, including {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}}, were known as the "Elegant Retainers" (風流侍従).<ref>『藤氏家伝』武智麻呂伝</ref>
In [[Tenpyō]] 11 (739), Sakurai was granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara|ja|大原氏}}, transitioning to commoner status. As {{ill|Treasurer|ja|大蔵卿}} in [[Tenpyō]] 16 (744), he was tasked with overseeing the {{ill|Kuni Palace|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu’s visit to the {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}. Later, he served as [[Danjō no In]].
He was still active during [[Empress Kōken]]'s reign in [[Tenpyō Shōhō]] 8 (756), evidenced by his poems included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}.<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref><ref name="oz">尾崎[2009: 10]</ref>
== Poetry ==
As a poet, Sakurai composed waka included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}. Notable poems include exchanges with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as {{ill|Governor of Tōtōmi|ja|遠江守}} around [[Tenpyō]] 5 (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a poem about {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around [[Tenpyō Shōhō]] 8 (756).<ref name="m4478" />
== Timeline ==
* [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従五位上) * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (正五位下) * [[Shinki]] 6 (729): Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]] (正五位上) * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下) * [[Tenpyō]] 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara * [[Tenpyō]] 16 (744): Appointed overseer of the {{ill|Kuni Palace|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu’s journey * Unknown: Appointed [[Treasurer|大蔵卿]] * Unknown: Appointed [[Danjō no In|弾正尹]]
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Son: Ishimoto (石本)<ref name="o" />
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Zoku Nihonki (Vol. 1)|publisher=Kodansha|year=1995|series=Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko}} * Murayama Izuru, "Notes on the 'Elegant Retainers' and Their Careers," ''Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin,'' Vol. 15, 1978. * Ozaki Tomoyoshi, "Exchanges Between Sakurai no Ōkimi and Emperor Shōmu," ''Tokoha Gakuen Junior College Bulletin,'' Vol. 40, 2009. * Hōga Toshio, ''Genealogical Compendium of Ancient Clans,'' Ancient Clans Research Association, 1986.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原 桜井) was a [[Nara period]] [[royal family|prince]], [[noble]], and [[waka poet]]. Originally named '''Sakurai no Ōkimi''' (桜井王), he adopted the name {{ill|Ōhara Clan|ja|大原氏}} upon relinquishing his royal status. He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『新撰姓氏録』左京皇別</ref><ref name="o">「音石山大僧都伝」『日本高僧伝要文抄』第3</ref> and the son of {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}}, a former {{ill|Governor-General of Tsukushi|ja|筑紫大宰帥}}. He held the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下) and served as [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹).
== Career ==
In [[Wadō]] 7 (714), Sakurai was promoted directly from no rank to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下). By [[Yōrō]] 5 (721), he reached [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従五位上). That same year, he served as a [[kago|palankin bearer]] alongside {{ill|Katsuragi no Ōkimi|ja|橘諸兄}} during the {{ill|ritual for Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王の卜定}} as the {{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.<ref>『政事要略』巻24</ref>
After [[Emperor Shōmu]]’s accession in [[Shinki]] 1 (724), Sakurai was appointed [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (正五位下). He continued his rise under Emperor Shōmu, reaching [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] in [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730). Around this period, Sakurai and his brothers, including {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}}, were known as the "Elegant Retainers" (風流侍従).<ref>『藤氏家伝』武智麻呂伝</ref>
In [[Tenpyō]] 11 (739), Sakurai was granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara|ja|大原氏}}, transitioning to commoner status. As {{ill|Treasurer|ja|大蔵卿}} in [[Tenpyō]] 16 (744), he was tasked with overseeing the {{ill|Kuni Palace|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu’s visit to the {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}. Later, he served as [[Danjō no In]].
He was still active during [[Empress Kōken]]'s reign in [[Tenpyō Shōhō]] 8 (756), evidenced by his poems included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}.<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref><ref name="oz">尾崎[2009: 10]</ref>
== Poetry ==
As a poet, Sakurai composed waka included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}. Notable poems include exchanges with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as {{ill|Governor of Tōtōmi|ja|遠江守}} around [[Tenpyō]] 5 (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a poem about {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around [[Tenpyō Shōhō]] 8 (756).<ref name="m4478" />

== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Son: Ishimoto (石本)<ref name="o" />

Originally named '''Sakurai no Ōkimi''' (桜井王), he adopted the name {{ill|Ōhara Clan|ja|大原氏}} upon relinquishing his royal status. He held the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下) and served as [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹).

Around this period, Sakurai and his brothers, including {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}}, were known as the "Elegant Retainers" (風流侍従).<ref>『藤氏家伝』武智麻呂伝</ref>
In [[Tenpyō]] 11 (739), Sakurai was granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara|ja|大原氏}}, transitioning to commoner status. As {{ill|Treasurer|ja|大蔵卿}} in [[Tenpyō]] 16 (744), he was tasked with overseeing the {{ill|Kuni Palace|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu’s visit to the {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}. Later, he served as [[Danjō no In]].
He was still active during [[Empress Kōken]]'s reign in [[Tenpyō Shōhō]] 8 (756), evidenced by his poems included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}.<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref><ref name="oz">尾崎[2009: 10]</ref>
== Poetry ==
As a poet, Sakurai composed waka included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}. Notable poems include exchanges with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as {{ill|Governor of Tōtōmi|ja|遠江守}} around [[Tenpyō]] 5 (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a poem about {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around [[Tenpyō Shōhō]] 8 (756).<ref name="m4478" />
== Timeline ==
* [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従五位上) * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (正五位下) * [[Shinki]] 6 (729): Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]] (正五位上) * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下) * [[Tenpyō]] 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara * [[Tenpyō]] 16 (744): Appointed overseer of the {{ill|Kuni Palace|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu’s journey * Unknown: Appointed [[Treasurer|大蔵卿]] * Unknown: Appointed [[Danjō no In|弾正尹]]
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Son: Ishimoto (石本)<ref name="o" />
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Zoku Nihonki (Vol. 1)|publisher=Kodansha|year=1995|series=Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko}} * Murayama Izuru, "Notes on the 'Elegant Retainers' and Their Careers," ''Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin,'' Vol. 15, 1978. * Ozaki Tomoyoshi, "Exchanges Between Sakurai no Ōkimi and Emperor Shōmu," ''Tokoha Gakuen Junior College Bulletin,'' Vol. 40, 2009. * Hōga Toshio, ''Genealogical Compendium of Ancient Clans,'' Ancient Clans Research Association, 1986.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原 桜井) was a [[Nara period]] [[royal family|prince]], [[noble]], and [[waka poet]]. Originally named '''Sakurai no Ōkimi''' (桜井王), he adopted the name {{ill|Ōhara Clan|ja|大原氏}} upon relinquishing his royal status. He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『新撰姓氏録』左京皇別</ref><ref name="o">「音石山大僧都伝」『日本高僧伝要文抄』第3</ref> and the son of {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}}, a former {{ill|Governor-General of Tsukushi|ja|筑紫大宰帥}}. He held the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下) and served as [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹).
== Career ==
In [[Wadō]] 7 (714), Sakurai was promoted directly from no rank to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下). By [[Yōrō]] 5 (721), he reached [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従五位上). That same year, he served as a [[kago|palankin bearer]] alongside {{ill|Katsuragi no Ōkimi|ja|橘諸兄}} during the {{ill|ritual for Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王の卜定}} as the {{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.<ref>『政事要略』巻24</ref>
After [[Emperor Shōmu]]’s accession in [[Shinki]] 1 (724), Sakurai was appointed [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (正五位下). He continued his rise under Emperor Shōmu, reaching [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] in [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730). Around this period, Sakurai and his brothers, including {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}}, were known as the "Elegant Retainers" (風流侍従).<ref>『藤氏家伝』武智麻呂伝</ref>
In [[Tenpyō]] 11 (739), Sakurai was granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara|ja|大原氏}}, transitioning to commoner status. As {{ill|Treasurer|ja|大蔵卿}} in [[Tenpyō]] 16 (744), he was tasked with overseeing the {{ill|Kuni Palace|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu’s visit to the {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}. Later, he served as [[Danjō no In]].
He was still active during [[Empress Kōken]]'s reign in [[Tenpyō Shōhō]] 8 (756), evidenced by his poems included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}.<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref><ref name="oz">尾崎[2009: 10]</ref>
== Poetry ==
As a poet, Sakurai composed waka included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}. Notable poems include exchanges with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as {{ill|Governor of Tōtōmi|ja|遠江守}} around [[Tenpyō]] 5 (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a poem about {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around [[Tenpyō Shōhō]] 8 (756).<ref name="m4478" />
== Timeline ==
* [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従五位下) * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従五位上) * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (正五位下) * [[Shinki]] 6 (729): Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]] (正五位上) * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下) * [[Tenpyō]] 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara * [[Tenpyō]] 16 (744): Appointed overseer of the {{ill|Kuni Palace|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu’s journey * Unknown: Appointed [[Treasurer|大蔵卿]] * Unknown: Appointed [[Danjō no In|弾正尹]]
== Family ==
* Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Son: Ishimoto (石本)<ref name="o" />
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Zoku Nihonki (Vol. 1)|publisher=Kodansha|year=1995|series=Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko}} * Murayama Izuru, "Notes on the 'Elegant Retainers' and Their Careers," ''Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin,'' Vol. 15, 1978. * Ozaki Tomoyoshi, "Exchanges Between Sakurai no Ōkimi and Emperor Shōmu," ''Tokoha Gakuen Junior College Bulletin,'' Vol. 40, 2009. * Hōga Toshio, ''Genealogical Compendium of Ancient Clans,'' Ancient Clans Research Association, 1986.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.
