In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==

== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.

During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.

By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />

Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>

During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.

Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />

'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下}}. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}

"Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). {{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />

== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />

That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />

== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.

=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
