{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the qqth year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō qq (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō qq (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the qqth year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō qq (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the qqth year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō qq (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the qqth year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō qq (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}

According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō qq (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />

In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.

=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} from no rank. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}

According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes ==

== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes ==

== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)|12=draft}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安|12=draft}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部|12=draft}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族|12=draft}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族|12=draft}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下|12=draft}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏|12=draft}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄|12=draft}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|12=draft}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=draft}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|12=draft}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅|12=draft}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|12=draft}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王|12=draft}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王|12=draft}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣|12=draft}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄|12=draft}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略|12=draft}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀|12=draft}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|12=draft}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=draft}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|12=draft}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部|12=draft}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝|12=draft}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人|12=draft}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿|12=draft}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京|12=draft}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|12=draft}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|12=draft}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集|12=draft}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝|12=draft}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇|12=draft}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|12=draft}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|12=draft}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|12=draft}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|12=draft}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=draft}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=draft}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

{{draft categories| [[Category:Ōhara clan]] [[Category:Demoted royal family members]] [[Category:Nara period male royals]] [[Category:Nara period aristocrats]] [[Category:Man'yō poets]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank honorees]] [[Category:7th-century births]] [[Category:8th-century deaths]] [[Category:Prince Sakurai]] [[Category:Ōhara Clan]] [[Category:Demoted royalty]] [[Category:Nara period princes]] [[Category:Nara period nobles]] [[Category:Man'yōshū poets]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:大原桜井]] [[sn:Prince Sakurai]] [[sn:rince Sakurai]]

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)|12=draft}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安|12=draft}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部|12=draft}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族|12=draft}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族|12=draft}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}}. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下|12=draft}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏|12=draft}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄|12=draft}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|12=draft}}. His official rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=draft}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|12=draft}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅|12=draft}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}} from no rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|12=draft}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王|12=draft}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王|12=draft}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣|12=draft}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄|12=draft}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略|12=draft}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀|12=draft}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|12=draft}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=draft}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|12=draft}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部|12=draft}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝|12=draft}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人|12=draft}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿|12=draft}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京|12=draft}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|12=draft}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|12=draft}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集|12=draft}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝|12=draft}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇|12=draft}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|12=draft}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|12=draft}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|12=draft}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|12=draft}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=draft}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=draft}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986. {{citation needed|おおはら の さくらい}}
{{citation needed|Prince Sakurai}}{{citation needed|Prince Sakurai}}{{citation needed|Prince Sakurai}}

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 大原桜井 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[奈良時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = 桜井王→大原桜井 | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[従四位|従四位下]][[弾正尹]] | 主君 = [[元明天皇]]→[[元正天皇]]→[[聖武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[大原氏|大原]][[真人]] | 父母 = 父：[[河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)|河内王]] | 兄弟 = [[大原高安|高安]]、'''桜井'''、[[大原門部|門部]] | 妻 = | 子 = 石本 }} '''大原 桜井'''（おおはら の さくらい）は、[[奈良時代]]の[[皇族]]・[[貴族]]・[[歌人]]。もと'''桜井王'''を称し、[[臣籍降下]]後の[[氏姓制度|氏姓]]は[[大原氏|大原]][[真人]]。[[敏達天皇]]の後裔<ref>『新撰姓氏録』左京皇別</ref><ref name="o">「音石山大僧都伝」『日本高僧伝要文抄』第3</ref>、[[大宰帥|筑紫大宰帥]]・[[河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)|河内王]]の子。[[官位]]は[[従四位|従四位下]]・[[弾正尹]]。
== 経歴 == [[和銅]]7年（[[714年]]）無位から[[従五位|従五位下]]に直叙される。[[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）従五位上。同年に[[井上内親王]]が[[斎王]]に卜定されて[[伊勢神宮]]への[[奉幣]]を行った際、[[橘諸兄|葛城王]]らと共に輿長を務めている<ref>『政事要略』巻24</ref>。
[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]）[[聖武天皇]]の即位後間もなく[[正五位|正五位下]]に叙せられると、神亀6年（[[729年]]）正五位上、[[天平]]3年（[[730年]]）[[従四位|従四位下]]と聖武朝前半にて順調に昇進した。またこの頃、[[侍従]]として聖武天皇に仕えた兄弟と想定される[[大原門部|門部王]]ら10余人と共に「風流侍従」と称された<ref>『藤氏家伝』武智麻呂伝</ref>。
天平11年（[[739年]]）兄弟の[[大原高安|高安王]]・門部王らと共に大原真人姓を賜与され臣籍降下する。[[大蔵卿]]在任時の天平16年（[[744年]]）には、聖武天皇の難波宮行幸に際して、[[恭仁京]]の留守司に任ぜられている。
のち、弾正尹も務め、『[[万葉集]]』に採録された[[和歌]]によると<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref>、[[孝謙天皇|孝謙]]朝の[[天平勝宝]]8歳（[[756年]]）頃まで存命であったとされる<ref name="oz">尾崎[2009: 10]</ref>。
== 人物 == 万葉歌人として、[[天平]]5年（[[732年]]）頃以降の<ref>村山[1978: 7]</ref>[[遠江国#国司|遠江守]]在任時に[[聖武天皇]]と贈答した和歌と<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref>、天平勝宝8歳（756年）頃に<ref name="oz" />[[佐保川]]の辺で詠んだ和歌<ref name="m4478" />が『万葉集』に採録されている。
== 官歴 == 注記のないものは『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *[[和銅]]7年（[[714年]]） 正月5日：[[従五位|従五位下]]（直叙） *[[養老]]5年（[[721年]]） 正月5日：従五位上 *[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]） 2月22日：[[正五位|正五位下]] *神亀6年（[[729年]]） 3月4日：正五位上 *[[天平]]3年（[[730年]]） 正月27日：[[従四位|従四位下]] *時期不詳：[[遠江国#国司|遠江守]]<ref name="m8" /> *天平11年（[[739年]]） 4月3日：[[大原氏|大原真人]]姓を賜与され[[臣籍降下]] *時期不詳：[[大蔵卿]] *天平16年（[[744年]]） 2月2日：[[恭仁京]]留守司 *時期不詳：[[弾正尹]]<ref name="o" />
== 系譜 == *父：[[河内王]]<ref>『本朝皇胤紹運録』</ref> *母：不詳 *妻：不詳 **男子：大原石本<ref name="o" />
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1995年 *村山出「「風流侍従」覚書 経歴の検討」『帯広大谷短期大学紀要 15』1978年 *尾崎富義「桜井王と聖武天皇の贈答歌」『常葉学園短期大学紀要 40』2009年 *[[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年

天平11年（[[739年]]）兄弟の[[大原高安|高安王]]・門部王らと共に大原真人姓を賜与され臣籍降下する。[[大蔵卿]]在任時の天平16年（[[744年]]）には、聖武天皇の難波宮行幸に際して、[[恭仁京]]の留守司に任ぜられている。

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, '''Sakurai''', {{ill|Ōhara Kadobe|ja|大原門部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|aristocrat|ja|貴族|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He was a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His official Rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of {{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from no Rank. In the 5th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. That same year, when {{ill|Princess Inoe|ja|井上内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was designated as a {{ill|saiō|ja|斎王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he served as a palanquin bearer along with {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|橘諸兄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (724), shortly after {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/demotion to commoner status|qq=}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara clan|qq=}} (Ōhara no Mahito). Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara Kōan|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōhara Kadohata|ja|大原門部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara Kadohata|qq=}}, Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『{{ill|Fujishi Kaden|ja|藤氏家伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishi Kaden|qq=}}』 武智麻呂伝</ref>

During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurakyō|qq=}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of {{ill|Kuni-kyō|ja|恭仁京|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-kyō|qq=}} during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}.

According to waka preserved in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Man'yōshū|qq=}},<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō Shōhō|qq=}} (756) during {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=}}'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
