revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi no Ōkimi|qq=}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara Takayasu|qq=}}, '''Sakurai''', [[:en:Prince Kadobe|Ōhara Kadobe]] | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/royal family member|qq=}}, [[:en:Nobility|aristocrat]], and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waka poet|qq=}} during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/demotion to commoner status|qq=}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara clan|qq=}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]<ref>『[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|qq=}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|qq=}}. His official Rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjon no in|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō]] (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} from no Rank. In the 5th year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. That same year, when [[:en:Princess Inoe|Princess Inoe]] was designated as a [[:en:Saiō|saiō]] and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering ceremony|qq=}} at [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], he served as a palanquin bearer along with [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Prince Katsuragi]].<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiji Yōryaku|qq=}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] (724), shortly after [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]]'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}}, and in the 3rd year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chamberlain|qq=}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara Kōan|qq=}} and [[:en:Prince Kadobe|Ōhara Kadohata]], Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『[[:en:Tōshi Kaden|Fujishi Kaden]]』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara clan|qq=}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurakyō|qq=}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of [[:en:Kuni-kyō|Kuni-kyō]] during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjon no in|qq=}}. According to waka preserved in the [[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]],<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] (756) during [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho River|qq=}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|qq=}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|qq=}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|qq=}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

"Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

[[ja:大原桜井]] [[sn:Prince Sakurai]] [[sn:rince Sakurai]]
<!--Prince Sakurai--> [[Category:大原氏]] [[Category:Princes of Japan in the Nara period]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:従四位下受位者]] [[Category:賜姓皇族]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi no Ōkimi|qq=}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara Takayasu|qq=}}, '''Sakurai''', [[:en:Prince Kadobe|Ōhara Kadobe]] | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/royal family member|qq=}}, [[:en:Nobility|aristocrat]], and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waka poet|qq=}} during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/demotion to commoner status|qq=}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara clan|qq=}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]<ref>『[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|qq=}}. His official Rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjon no in|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō]] (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} from no Rank. In the 5th year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. That same year, when [[:en:Princess Inoe|Princess Inoe]] was designated as a [[:en:Saiō|saiō]] and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering ceremony|qq=}} at [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], he served as a palanquin bearer along with [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Prince Katsuragi]].<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiji Yōryaku|qq=}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] (724), shortly after [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]]'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}, and in the 3rd year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chamberlain|qq=}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara Kōan|qq=}} and [[:en:Prince Kadobe|Ōhara Kadohata]], Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『[[:en:Tōshi Kaden|Fujishi Kaden]]』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurakyō|qq=}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of [[:en:Kuni-kyō|Kuni-kyō]] during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjon no in|qq=}}. According to waka preserved in the [[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]],<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] (756) during [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho River|qq=}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|qq=}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|qq=}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|qq=}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi no Ōkimi|qq=}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara Takayasu|qq=}}, '''Sakurai''', [[:en:Prince Kadobe|Ōhara Kadobe]] | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/royal family member|qq=}}, [[:en:Nobility|aristocrat]], and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waka poet|qq=}} during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/demotion to commoner status|qq=}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara clan|qq=}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]<ref>『[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|qq=}}. His official Rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjon no in|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō]] (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} from no Rank. In the 5th year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. That same year, when [[:en:Princess Inoe|Princess Inoe]] was designated as a [[:en:Saiō|saiō]] and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering ceremony|qq=}} at [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], he served as a palanquin bearer along with [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Prince Katsuragi]].<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiji Yōryaku|qq=}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] (724), shortly after [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]]'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}, and in the 3rd year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chamberlain|qq=}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara Kōan|qq=}} and [[:en:Prince Kadobe|Ōhara Kadohata]], Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『[[:en:Tōshi Kaden|Fujishi Kaden]]』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurakyō|qq=}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of [[:en:Kuni-kyō|Kuni-kyō]] during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjon no in|qq=}}. According to waka preserved in the [[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]],<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] (756) during [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho River|qq=}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|qq=}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|qq=}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|qq=}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.
{{translated page|ja|大原桜井|version=99376344|comment=Imported full ja history}}

His official Rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjon no in|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō]] (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} from no Rank. In the 5th year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. That same year, when [[:en:Princess Inoe|Princess Inoe]] was designated as a [[:en:Saiō|saiō]] and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering ceremony|qq=}} at [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], he served as a palanquin bearer along with [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Prince Katsuragi]].<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiji Yōryaku|qq=}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] (724), shortly after [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]]'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}, and in the 3rd year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chamberlain|qq=}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara Kōan|qq=}} and [[:en:Prince Kadobe|Ōhara Kadohata]], Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『[[:en:Tōshi Kaden|Fujishi Kaden]]』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurakyō|qq=}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of [[:en:Kuni-kyō|Kuni-kyō]] during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjon no in|qq=}}. According to waka preserved in the [[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]],<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] (756) during [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho River|qq=}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|qq=}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|qq=}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|qq=}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}, and in the 3rd year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chamberlain|qq=}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara Kōan|qq=}} and [[:en:Prince Kadobe|Ōhara Kadohata]], Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『[[:en:Tōshi Kaden|Fujishi Kaden]]』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurakyō|qq=}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of [[:en:Kuni-kyō|Kuni-kyō]] during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjon no in|qq=}}. According to waka preserved in the [[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]],<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] (756) during [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho River|qq=}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|qq=}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|qq=}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes ==

== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|qq=}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi no Ōkimi|qq=}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara Takayasu|qq=}}, '''Sakurai''', [[:en:Prince Kadobe|Ōhara Kadobe]] | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/royal family member|qq=}}, [[:en:Nobility|aristocrat]], and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waka poet|qq=}} during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/demotion to commoner status|qq=}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara clan|qq=}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]<ref>『[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|qq=}}. His official Rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjon no in|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō]] (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} from no Rank. In the 5th year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. That same year, when [[:en:Princess Inoe|Princess Inoe]] was designated as a [[:en:Saiō|saiō]] and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering ceremony|qq=}} at [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], he served as a palanquin bearer along with [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Prince Katsuragi]].<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiji Yōryaku|qq=}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] (724), shortly after [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]]'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}, and in the 3rd year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chamberlain|qq=}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara Kōan|qq=}} and [[:en:Prince Kadobe|Ōhara Kadohata]], Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『[[:en:Tōshi Kaden|Fujishi Kaden]]』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurakyō|qq=}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of [[:en:Kuni-kyō|Kuni-kyō]] during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjon no in|qq=}}. According to waka preserved in the [[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]],<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] (756) during [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho River|qq=}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]. * [[Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|qq=}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|qq=}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes ==

== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho River|qq=}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]. * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|qq=}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|qq=}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|qq=}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

[[ja:大原桜井]] [[sn:Prince Sakurai]] [[sn:rince Sakurai]]
<!--Prince Sakurai--> [[Category:Ōhara Clan]] [[Category:Princes of Japan in the Nara period]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:Recipients of Lower Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Imperial Princes Granted Surnames]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi no Ōkimi|qq=}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara Takayasu|qq=}}, '''Sakurai''', [[:en:Prince Kadobe|Ōhara Kadobe]] | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/royal family member|qq=}}, [[:en:Nobility|aristocrat]], and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waka poet|qq=}} during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/demotion to commoner status|qq=}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara clan|qq=}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]<ref>『[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|qq=}}. His official Rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjon no in|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō]] (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} from no Rank. In the 5th year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. That same year, when [[:en:Princess Inoe|Princess Inoe]] was designated as a [[:en:Saiō|saiō]] and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering ceremony|qq=}} at [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], he served as a palanquin bearer along with [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Prince Katsuragi]].<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiji Yōryaku|qq=}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] (724), shortly after [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]]'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}, and in the 3rd year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chamberlain|qq=}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara Kōan|qq=}} and [[:en:Prince Kadobe|Ōhara Kadohata]], Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『[[:en:Tōshi Kaden|Fujishi Kaden]]』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurakyō|qq=}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of [[:en:Kuni-kyō|Kuni-kyō]] during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjon no in|qq=}}. According to waka preserved in the [[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]],<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] (756) during [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho River|qq=}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]. * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|qq=}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|qq=}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|qq=}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi no Ōkimi|qq=}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara Takayasu|qq=}}, '''Sakurai''', [[:en:Prince Kadobe|Ōhara Kadobe]] | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/royal family member|qq=}}, [[:en:Nobility|aristocrat]], and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waka poet|qq=}} during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/demotion to commoner status|qq=}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara clan|qq=}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]<ref>『[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|qq=}}. His official Rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjon no in|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō]] (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} from no Rank. In the 5th year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. That same year, when [[:en:Princess Inoe|Princess Inoe]] was designated as a [[:en:Saiō|saiō]] and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering ceremony|qq=}} at [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], he served as a palanquin bearer along with [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Prince Katsuragi]].<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiji Yōryaku|qq=}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] (724), shortly after [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]]'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}, and in the 3rd year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chamberlain|qq=}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara Kōan|qq=}} and [[:en:Prince Kadobe|Ōhara Kadohata]], Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『[[:en:Tōshi Kaden|Fujishi Kaden]]』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurakyō|qq=}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of [[:en:Kuni-kyō|Kuni-kyō]] during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjon no in|qq=}}. According to waka preserved in the [[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]],<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] (756) during [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho River|qq=}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]. * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[:en:Shinki|Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|qq=}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|qq=}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|qq=}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.
{{translated page|ja|大原桜井|version=99376344|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q21015219]] Wikidata name: Sakurai Ohara [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Ōhara Sakurai | image = | caption = | era = [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従四位下), [[Danjō no In|Danjō no In]] (弾正尹) | master = [[Empress Genmei]], [[Empress Genshō]], [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Ōhara Clan|Ōhara]] (大原真人) | parents = Father: {{ill|Kawachi no Ōkimi|ja|河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi no Ōkimi|qq=}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōhara Takayasu|ja|大原高安|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara Takayasu|qq=}}, '''Sakurai''', [[:en:Prince Kadobe|Ōhara Kadobe]] | spouse = Unknown | children = Ishimoto (石本) }}
'''Ōhara Sakurai''' (大原桜井) was a {{ill|royal family member|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/royal family member|qq=}}, [[:en:Nobility|aristocrat]], and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waka poet|qq=}} during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/demotion to commoner status|qq=}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara clan|qq=}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]<ref>『[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|qq=}}. His official Rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjon no in|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō]] (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} from no Rank. In the 5th year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. That same year, when [[:en:Princess Inoe|Princess Inoe]] was designated as a [[:en:Saiō|saiō]] and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering ceremony|qq=}} at [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], he served as a palanquin bearer along with [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Prince Katsuragi]].<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiji Yōryaku|qq=}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] (724), shortly after [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]]'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}, and in the 3rd year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chamberlain|qq=}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara Kōan|qq=}} and [[:en:Prince Kadobe|Ōhara Kadohata]], Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『[[:en:Tōshi Kaden|Fujishi Kaden]]』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurakyō|qq=}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of [[:en:Kuni-kyō|Kuni-kyō]] during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjon no in|qq=}}. According to waka preserved in the [[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]],<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] (756) during [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho River|qq=}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]. * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[:en:Shinki|Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|qq=}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|qq=}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|qq=}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.

Initially known as '''Sakurai-Ō''' (桜井王), he adopted the name '''Ōhara Sakurai''' after his {{ill|demotion to commoner status|ja|臣籍降下|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/demotion to commoner status|qq=|1=shinseki kōka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193495}}, taking the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara clan|qq=|1=Ōhara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433162}} (Ōhara no Mahito). He was a descendant of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]<ref>『[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』Left Capital, Imperial Lineage</ref><ref name="o">"Onishiyama Daisōzu-den" in 『{{ill|Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|ja|日本高僧伝要文抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kōsōden Yōbunshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』Vol. 3</ref>, and the son of {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|qq=|1=Kawachi no O|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209039}}. His official Rank was {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjon no in|qq=|1=Danjon no in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487787}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Career === In the 7th year of [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō]] (714), Sakurai was directly promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} from no Rank. In the 5th year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (721), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. That same year, when [[:en:Princess Inoe|Princess Inoe]] was designated as a [[:en:Saiō|saiō]] and performed the {{ill|offering ceremony|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering ceremony|qq=|1=Hōhei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445508}} at [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], he served as a palanquin bearer along with [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Prince Katsuragi]].<ref>『{{ill|Seiji Yōryaku|ja|政事要略|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiji Yōryaku|qq=|1=Seiji Yōryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499054}}』Vol. 24</ref>
=== Under Emperor Shōmu === In the 1st year of [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] (724), shortly after [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]]'s accession, Sakurai was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}. By the 6th year of Shinki (729), he attained {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}, and in the 3rd year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] (730), he reached {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. During this time, he served as {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chamberlain|qq=|1=Chamberlain of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2948088}} to Emperor Shōmu. Alongside his siblings, including {{ill|Ōhara Kōan|ja|大原高安|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara Kōan|qq=|1=Takayasu Ohara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17989661}} and [[:en:Prince Kadobe|Ōhara Kadohata]], Sakurai was one of the "refined chamberlains" (風流侍従).<ref>『[[:en:Tōshi Kaden|Fujishi Kaden]]』 武智麻呂伝</ref>
In the 11th year of Tenpyō (739), Sakurai and his siblings, Kōan and Kadohata, were granted the surname {{ill|Ōhara clan|ja|大原真人|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, marking their demotion to commoner status. During his tenure as {{ill|Ōkurakyō|ja|大蔵卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurakyō|qq=|1=Ōkurakyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539334}} in the 16th year of Tenpyō (744), he was assigned as the deputy administrator of [[:en:Kuni-kyō|Kuni-kyō]] during Emperor Shōmu's visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=|1=Naniwa Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3608523}}.
=== Later Life === Later, Sakurai served as {{ill|Danjon no in|ja|弾正尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjon no in|qq=|1=Danjon no in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487787}}. According to waka preserved in the [[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]],<ref name="m4478">『万葉集』巻20-4478</ref> he lived until at least the 8th year of [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] (756) during [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]]'s reign.<ref name="oz">Ozaki [2009: 10]</ref>
== Literary Contributions == As a poet, Sakurai's works are preserved in the Man'yōshū. These include a waka exchanged with Emperor Shōmu during his tenure as the governor of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] in the 5th year of Tenpyō (732)<ref name="m8">『万葉集』巻8-1614</ref> and a waka composed near the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho River|qq=}} around 756.<ref name="m4478" />
== Career Timeline == Details not explicitly noted are from the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]. * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 7 (714): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion). * [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 5 (721): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[:en:Shinki|Shinki]] 1 (724): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (February 22). * Shinki 6 (729): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (March 4). * [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 3 (730): Promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (January 27). * Date unknown: Appointed governor of Tōtōmi Province.<ref name="m8" /> * Tenpyō 11 (739): Granted the surname Ōhara no Mahito (April 3). * Date unknown: Appointed Ōkurakyō. * Tenpyō 16 (744): Appointed deputy administrator of Kuni-kyō (February 2). * Date unknown: Appointed Danjon no in.<ref name="o" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|ja|河内王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-Ō (Asuka period)|qq=|1=Kawachi no O|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209039}}<ref>『{{ill|Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|qq=}}』</ref> * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ** Son: Ōhara Ishimoto<ref name="o" />
== Notes ==

== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}. 『続日本紀（上）』 {{ill|Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdansha Gakujutsu Bunko|qq=|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}, 1995. * Murayama, Ide. "Notes on the 'Refined Chamberlains' and Their Careers." 『Obihiro Otani Junior College Bulletin』15, 1978. * Ozaki, Tomiyoshi. "Poems Exchanged Between Sakurai-Ō and Emperor Shōmu." 『Tokiwa Gakuen Junior College Bulletin』40, 2009. * Hōga, Toshio. 『Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies』 Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.
